# actiquantiles

`actiquantiles` maps physical activity values to NHANES-based quantiles.
The package exposes an `acti_`-prefixed wrapper around
[`mapnhanespa::map_nhanes_pa_quantiles()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/mapnhanespa/man/map_nhanes_pa_quantiles.html)
so the public API can stay stable while new mapping backends are added
later.

Core entry points:

- [`acti_map_nhanes()`](http://jhuwit.github.io/actiquantiles/reference/acti_map_nhanes.md)
  for participant-level quantile mapping
- [`mapnhanespa::nhanes_pa_quantile()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/mapnhanespa/man/nhanes_pa_quantile.html)
  for a single value lookup
- [`mapnhanespa::nhanes_pa_age_category()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/mapnhanespa/man/nhanes_pa_age_category.html)
  for age binning

## Installation

You can install `actiquantiles` from GitHub with:

``` r

# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("jhuwit/actiquantiles")
```

The package depends on `mapnhanespa`, which provides the NHANES mapping
tables and lookup logic.

## Quick Start

``` r

example_data <- data.frame(
  id = c("A", "B"),
  age = c(25, 62),
  sex = c("Female", "Male"),
  measure = c("mims", "ssl_steps"),
  value = c(15000, 7500)
)

mapped <- acti_map_nhanes(example_data)
mapped
#>   id age    sex   measure value acti_pa_quantile
#> 1  A  25 Female      mims 15000        0.5349443
#> 2  B  62   Male ssl_steps  7500        0.3527381
```

The wrapper keeps the input columns and adds `acti_pa_quantile`:

``` r

head(mapped)
#>   id age    sex   measure value acti_pa_quantile
#> 1  A  25 Female      mims 15000        0.5349443
#> 2  B  62   Male ssl_steps  7500        0.3527381
```

For a single value, use the scalar helper from `mapnhanespa` directly:

``` r

mapnhanespa::nhanes_pa_quantile(
  value = 15000,
  age = 25,
  sex = "Female",
  measure = "mims"
)
#> [1] 0.5349443
```

# Package index

## All functions

- [`acti_map_nhanes()`](http://jhuwit.github.io/actiquantiles/reference/acti_map_nhanes.md)
  : Map physical activity values to NHANES population quantiles
